title: meeting-radar configuration
description: Schema for meeting-radar.config.yaml at the target repo's root. Every field is optional; the skill falls back to the documented defaults (see config.example.yaml, reference/sources.md, reference/detection.md) when the file or a field is absent.

providerInterface.description: How to talk to a configured source. MCP or CLI is recommended; api is only appropriate when the API is well-documented and public. Same shape used by meeting-to-artifacts and knowledge-management.

kind.description: mcp/cli are preferred — no bespoke integration to maintain. api requires apiDocsUrl and should only be used when no MCP/CLI integration exists.

server.description: Required when kind is mcp: the configured MCP server name (e.g. gmail, google-calendar, outlook).

command.description: Required when kind is cli: the CLI invocation template.

apiDocsUrl.description: Required when kind is api: link to the source's public API documentation. Do not configure kind: api against an undocumented/private API.

sourceName.description: Free-form source name, harness-interpreted (no fixed enum, per decision-007), e.g. google-calendar, outlook, gmail. If it names a source with no working interface configured/available, the harness should say so and ask rather than guessing an integration.

sources.description: The two input systems radar scans. Either may be omitted — radar works from whichever is configured (calendar-only, email-only, or both).

calendar.description: A calendar system plus how to reach it.

calendars.description: Which calendars to scan. Default: the primary calendar only.

email.description: A mailbox plus how to reach it.

mailboxes.description: Which mailboxes/labels to scan. Default: INBOX.

scan.description: What window to scan and how to recognize a meeting-related email.

window.description: Lookback window, e.g. 7d, 48h, 2w. How far back to scan email and calendar.

signals.description: Heuristics that mark an email as meeting-related. See reference/detection.md.

senders.description: Sender addresses/domains that indicate a recording or summary email.

subjectPatterns.description: Subject substrings/patterns that indicate a meeting recording, transcript, or summary.

recordingLinkHosts.description: Hosts whose links, when present in a body, count as a recording/transcript.

correlation.description: How email signals are matched to calendar events.

matchWindowHours.description: Maximum gap between an event's time and an email's timestamp for the two to be correlated into the same candidate.

handoff.description: Where approved candidates go and how re-processing is prevented.

skill.description: The downstream skill each approved candidate is handed to (at its Ingest step).

meetingArtifactsConfig.description: Path to the meeting-to-artifacts config (meeting-artifacts.config.yaml), read to reuse its `series` registry when correlating and to know its output tree for dedup.

processedLog.description: Append-only ledger of already-handed-off meeting sources (metadata + links only, never message contents), used to dedup on subsequent scans.

privacy.description: Controls how much email radar reads and whether any handoff is automatic. See reference/privacy.md.

reviewGate.description: always: the user confirms every candidate before handoff. auto-known-series: occurrences of a known `series` may be pre-selected in the digest, but the digest is still shown and remains deselectable — never a silent auto-ingest.

bodyAccess.description: metadata-first: read headers/subjects/senders to detect candidates; open a full body only to extract a link/summary from a message already matched as a candidate. full: bodies may be read during detection (use only when the mailbox is dedicated to meeting notifications).
